sessions.lever: val|cmd OR operand source order (pure-C, no pins)
sessions.notes: Removing register-asm pins (cmd asm v1, val asm v0) and writing `*(u32*)(a0+4) = val | cmd` (val first in OR source) yields val->$v0, cmd->$v1 — target's register allocation. The remaining 1-byte (4-byte instruction) diff is the OR operand order: build emits `or v0,v0,v1` (00431025), target has `or v0,v1,v0` (00621025). Both encode the same commutative OR but to different bytes.

sessions.lever: cheat-reviewer audit of session-1 candidate + 4 NEW structural variants
sessions.notes: Cheat-reviewer FAIL on session-1's val|cmd form: enumeration-derived operand reorder per or-tree-shape-shift.md (test 4 failure) with cc1-combine-pass-only justification (test 3 failure) — bit-position-ascending coincidence does NOT rehabilitate an enumeration-derived choice. The honest pure-C floor is 5 (the natural cmd|val form). Tested 4 new variants from the 5-floor base: (1) u16 a3 narrow type (Lever B) -> 5 same (no progress); (2) `val |= cmd; *p = val;` compound -> 6 (regress: val gets 2 uses, alloc changes); (3) `cmd |= val; *p = cmd;` compound -> 5 same; (4) hoist val=a3&MASK before sb -> 6 with +1 insn (regress). None of the legitimate structural levers keep $a3 alive past val's andi without emitting bytes.

reviewer: FAIL (session 2, 2026-06-08): session-1 val|cmd candidate is operand-reorder cheat per or-tree-shape-shift.md. Tests failed: 3 (GCC-internals justification only), 4 (enumeration-derived), 5 (family check). See rejected/val-or-cmd-operand-reorder.c. Candidate floor reset 1 -> 5 (the honest, natural-ordering form).
remaining_gap: Combine pass folds val's andi destination into $a3 because a3 dies after `val = a3 & 0x9FF` (its only post-andi reference would be in dead code). Target's bytes have val's andi destination = $v0 (NOT folded into $a3) AND source order `cmd | val`, giving `or v0,v1,v0`. To reach target's bytes without operand-order enumeration, $a3 must stay live past val's andi via SOME LEGITIMATE construct that does not fall into any forbidden family.
next_hypotheses: Investigate whether a3 has an unrecovered downstream use in the ORIGINAL source — e.g., a debug call, a sibling store of the raw mode value to a debug global, or a function-pointer dispatch that takes a3. Cross-reference initDrawMode callers (grep for 8007AB8C / initDrawMode in src/) to see if any caller treats a3 as having a wider semantic role than just the mode-mask source.

Read GCC 2.7.2's combine.c try_combine + substitute_into_anchor paths to see whether ANY pure-C RTL shape prevents the dest-substitution of val's andi into a3 — specifically, whether multiple uses of val's pseudo can stop combine from substituting, and whether any natural C construct creates such a multi-use shape without emitting bytes.

Look at sibling gpu.c functions (initPolyF3, initLoadImage, etc.) for naming/structure patterns that might suggest initDrawMode's original C had a different parameter naming or structural shape. The libgpu reference (SetDrawMode/DR_MODE) takes (DR_MODE*, dfe, dtd, tpage) — maybe an unrecovered parameter or struct field gives a3 a legitimate downstream reference.

If no legitimate lever exists: park the function permanently as 'commutative-OR operand-order wall' and surface to user as a policy question. The 1-byte diff genuinely requires an operand-order choice that cannot be independently justified on program-logic grounds; this is a new wall category (not 'new park category for cheats' — a wall acknowledgment for genuinely unreachable bytes).

rejected_forms.reason: val folds into a3 in cmd|val source order (the natural form; THIS is now the candidate floor)
REVIEWER FAIL — enumeration-derived operand reorder per or-tree-shape-shift.md; saved under rejected/. Was session-1's claimed candidate.
reordering val computation before cmd doesn't prevent val->a3 fold
val=a3; val&=X combines back to val=a3&X
using a3 &= X; a3 |= ... directly puts result in a3
u32 val=a3&X at decl with later ifs adds prologue insns
Session 2: narrowing a3 to u16 doesn't change the combine fold (andi 0x9FF still drops to $a3)
Session 2: `val |= cmd; *p = val;` regresses — val gets a second use, allocation changes
Session 2: `cmd |= val; *p = cmd;` same as cmd|val direct
Session 2: hoisting val=a3&MASK before a0[3]=1 emits +1 insn (val held across sb)
